title: FormalJets
summary: Represents a finite rho-jet of the tangential metric g_rho and the condition that it solves the FG Ricci equations to finite order.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Represents a finite rho-jet of the tangential metric g_rho and the condition that it solves the FG Ricci equations to finite order.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/FG/FormalJets.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A component-level symmetric two-tensor expression. In explicit terms, for ι a type, its defining expression is ι maps to ι maps to the real numbers.
Symmetry predicate for component-level two-tensors. In explicit terms, for t of type sym2 component ι, its defining expression is for every i j : ι, T i j equals T j i.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{SymmetricComponent}(T)=\forall i j \colon ι, T i j = T j i

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A finite formal metric jet g_rho = g + rho g^(1) +... + rho^K g^(K). Its mathematical data are: coeff: Fin K plus 1 maps to Sym2 Component ι; base Metric: Sym2 Component ι; schouten: Sym2 Component ι; coeff zero eq base: for every i j : ι, coeff ⟨0, Nat.succ pos K⟩ i j equals base Metric i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{coeff}&\colon \operatorname{Fin} (K + 1) \longrightarrow \operatorname{Sym2Component} ι\\\operatorname{baseMetric}&\colon \operatorname{Sym2Component} ι\\\operatorname{schouten}&\colon \operatorname{Sym2Component} ι\\\operatorname{coeff\_zero\_eq\_base}&\colon \forall i j \colon ι, \operatorname{coeff} ⟨0, \mathbb{N}.\operatorname{succ\_pos} K⟩ i j = \operatorname{baseMetric} i j\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Access the coefficient g^(m) using a proof that m <= K. In explicit terms, for j of type formal metric jet ι k, m in the natural numbers, and hm of type m is at most k, its defining expression is J.coeff ⟨m, Nat.lt succ of le hm⟩.
correspondence.leanType: def coeffAt (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≤ K) : Sym2Component ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For J of type Formal Metric Jet ι K, J.coeff At 0 Nat.zero le K equals J.base Metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem coeffAt_zero (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) : J.coeffAt 0 (Nat.zero_le K) = J.baseMetric

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first jet coefficient, available when 1 <= K. In explicit terms, for j of type formal metric jet ι k, and hk of type 1 is at most k, its defining expression is J.coeff At 1 h K.
correspondence.leanType: def firstCoeff (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (hK : 1 ≤ K) : Sym2Component ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Finite-order FG Ricci conditions for a formal metric jet. At order at least one, the first-jet relation is recorded as a field. Later files can refine this structure by replacing the field with a proof from the normal-form Ricci equations. Its mathematical data are: order le: order is at most K; first jet eq two schouten: for every horder : 1 is at most order, for every i j : ι, J.coeff At 1 le trans horder order le i j equals 2 times J.schouten i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{order\_le}&\colon \operatorname{order} \le K\\\operatorname{first\_jet\_eq\_two\_schouten}&\colon \forall \operatorname{horder} \colon 1 \le \operatorname{order}, \forall i j \colon ι, J.\operatorname{coeffAt} 1 (\operatorname{le\_trans} \operatorname{horder} \operatorname{order\_le}) i j = 2 \cdot J.\operatorname{schouten} i j\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (order : ℕ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first FG jet relation g'|_{rho=0} = 2 P. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,J\colon \operatorname{FormalMetricJet} ι K,\;H\colon \operatorname{SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder} J 1,\;i,j\colon ι,\quad J.\operatorname{coeffAt} 1 H.\operatorname{order\_le} i j = 2 \cdot J.\operatorname{schouten} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem FG_first_jet (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (H : SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder J 1) (i j : ι) : J.coeffAt 1 H.order_le i j = 2 * J.schouten i j

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
